what_it_does: An acceptance test script for the Convergence X7 feature: it exercises Suite.pin to set and unset a pinned flag on annotation and chat records, verifies the flag persists on disk and is overlaid by annotations_at/chats_at, checks that a pinned item is held inside the bounded R2 window via the existing pin_bonus while the cap and ranking formula remain unchanged, and tests the production clicked-comment path where pinning interacts with dedup.
observations: Top-level script with a global PASS counter and a custom check(label, cond) helper.
Uses tempfile FsStore instances, NodeRegistry discovery, and shutil cleanup.
Tests pin persistence by creating a second Suite over the same store root.
Calls internal Suite methods _r2_gather and _r2_score_and_cap directly.
Includes a baseline check that an old unpinned item is evicted by the budget cap.
Verifies the pin bonus equals exactly R2_PIN_WEIGHT via _r2_score.
Uses expect_raises to assert loud failure for non-existent items and malformed addresses.
Covers chat pinning through chats_at overlay.
Contains an X7×X8 scenario with annotation+chat+event dedup and a single pinned survivor.
standouts: Framed as reviving a 'dead term' in _r2_score by adding the missing SET path.
Verifies pin state survives Suite reload from disk.
Verifies the bounded R2 window still enforces its budget after pinning.
Includes a cross-feature production path test (clicked comment dedup + pin).
conventions: Uses sys.path.insert and COMPANY_TEST_RUN environment flag.
Follows a simple assertion helper pattern rather than pytest/unittest.
Uses lambda wrappers to pass raising callables into expect_raises.
Cleans up temporary directories in a finally block.
concerns: expect_raises catches any Exception, so it could pass on unintended errors.
Relies on private/internal methods (_r2_gather, _r2_score_and_cap) for assertions.
Hardcodes paths, node directory, and locus strings.
Assumes text-length budget behavior without documenting R2_BUDGET size.
Multiple datetime.now calls could introduce ordering assumptions.
notes: A behavioral contract test that turns the existing R2 pin_bonus from unreachable code into an observable, persisted operator override.
questions: What are the exact values of R2_BUDGET and R2_PIN_WEIGHT?
How does Suite.pin locate and mutate the underlying record?
What constitutes a malformed address for the S0 gate?
How does _r2_score_and_cap break ties when scores are equal?
What does the X8 ingest_comment/dedup implementation actually do?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to prove the pin override works end-to-end while preserving the existing R2 formula, and the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: To wire the operator's 'keep this in view' action into the convergence retrieval pipeline without changing the scoring formula, and to demonstrate it works across persistence, reload, dedup, and budget constraints.
novelty: It treats an acceptance test as a research-synthesis document that diagnoses a dead term in the scoring formula and then proves the SET path makes it reachable.
contribution: A concrete, file-local behavioral contract for pin/unpin: persistence on the open record, overlay reads, bounded-window retention, cap preservation, loud failure on missing items, unchanged R2 scoring formula, and compatibility with the clicked-comment dedup path.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test that verifies Suite.pin makes the existing R2 pin_bonus reachable by persisting a pinned flag on annotations and chats, holding pinned items in the bounded R2 window while preserving the scoring formula and budget cap.
intention_for_embedding: To validate the X7 pin override end-to-end, including persistence, reload, unpin, loud failure, chat support, formula preservation, and interaction with the X8 clicked-comment dedup path.

symbols.does: Asserts a condition and increments a global pass counter, printing an 'ok' line.
symbols.description: A lightweight test assertion helper used throughout the script; it updates the global PASS variable and fails with a labeled message when the condition is false.

symbols.does: Runs a callable and passes only if it raises any exception.
symbols.description: A helper that catches any Exception from the supplied function and counts it as a passing check, failing if no exception is raised.
